mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 4.5 percent within a single month, it signals labor market deterioration that typically precedes earnings revisions downward, depressing S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in more than 60 percent of triggered instances despite the negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts combined with falling industrial production indicates demand outpacing supply, creating inflationary pressure that forces the Federal Reserve to maintain restrictive policy, negatively impacting equity valuations over 60 days.
failure_condition: Joint signal fails to predict negative 60d forward returns in at least 50 percent of out-of-sample windows.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-rate environment, a contraction in commercial and industrial credit demand accelerates liquidity withdrawal, compressing equity multiples and driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Credit contraction in high-rate periods does not produce statistically significant negative equity returns.

mechanism_narrative: Sequential quarterly growth in real gross domestic product reflects underlying economic momentum that translates into higher corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in expansionary quarters are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When monthly employment growth exceeds 200 thousand while the unemployment rate stays below 4.0 percent, it signals a tight labor market that sustains wage-driven consumption, supporting equity valuations over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint labor strength signal fails to predict positive 5d forward returns in at least 50 percent of out-of-sample windows.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of elevated inflation, industrial production growth becomes a critical proxy for capacity utilization; accelerating output signals supply-side resilience that offsets rate fears, boosting S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration in high-inflation regimes does not produce statistically significant positive equity returns.

mechanism_narrative: Steady month-over-month increases in total nonfarm payrolls reflect consistent job creation that supports consumer spending and corporate revenue growth, yielding positive S&P 500 returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in positive employment months are not significantly higher than baseline.
